perturbation.original_text: (a) "Affiliate" means a person or entity that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, or is controlled by, or is under common control with, a Party. The term 'control' means the possession of the power to direct the management and policies of the person or entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) "Affiliate" means a person or entity that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls, or is controlled by, or is under common control with, a Party. The term 'control' means having a business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition includes explicit terms like 'ownership of voting securities' and 'contract'. The change to 'having a business relationship' lacks a clear legal threshold for determining control, creating ambiguity that could be challenged in court.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified definition contradicts the established SEC definition of 'control' in securities law, which focuses on the power to direct management and policies, whether through ownership, contract, or otherwise. The contract's revised definition omits necessary elements such as voting securities or contractual agreements, which creates legal uncertainty.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.12b-2 - Definitions. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.12b-2 - Definitions. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.12b-2 Definitions. Unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms, when used in the rules contained in this regulation or in Regulation 13A or 15D or in the forms for statements and reports filed pursuant to sections 12, 13 or 15(d) of the act, shall have the respective meanings indicated in this rule: Accelerated filer and large accelerated filer —(1) Accelerated filer. The term accelerated filer means an issuer after it first meets the following conditions as of the end of its fiscal year: (i) The issuer had an aggregate worldwide market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by its non-affiliates of $75 million or more, but less than $700 million, as of the last business day of the issuer 's most recently completed second fiscal quarter; (ii) The issuer has been subject to the requirements of section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78m or 78o(d) ) for a period of at least twelve calendar months; and (iii) The issuer has filed at least one annual report pursuant to section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Act; and (iv) The issuer is not eligible to use the requirements for smaller reporting companies under the revenue test in paragraph (2) or (3)(iii)(B) of the “smaller reporting company” definition in this section , as applicable. (2) Large accelerated filer. The term large accelerated filer means an issuer after it first meets the following conditions as of the end of its fiscal year: (i) The issuer had an aggregate worldwide market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by its non-affiliates of $700 million or more, as of the last business day of the issuer 's most recently completed second fiscal quarter; (ii) The issuer has been subject to the requirements of section 13(a) o
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the established SEC definition of 'control' by replacing specific criteria like 'ownership of voting securities' and 'contract' with the vague term 'having a business relationship.' This introduces ambiguity, making it difficult to determine control under securities law, as described in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 17 CFR § 240.12b-2, defines terms related to issuers and their market value, indicating the importance of precise definitions in securities regulations. The altered definition weakens the legally enforceable meaning of 'control', making the contract potentially unenforceable because it deviates from legally recognized definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising under or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by arbitration under the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association, with the venue of any such arbitration proceeding to be in Santa Clara, California or such other location as maybe agreed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute arising under or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by arbitration under rules determined solely by the Forty Niners SC, with the venue of any such arbitration proceeding to be in Santa Clara, California or such other location as Forty Niners SC decides.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the arbitration rules and venue to be determined solely by one party introduces bias and contradicts the principles of fair and impartial arbitration, making the clause potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification contradicts the FAA's intent to provide a framework for fair and impartial arbitration by allowing one party (Forty Niners SC) to unilaterally determine the arbitration rules and venue. This level of control raises concerns about procedural fairness and could render the arbitration agreement unenforceable.

CHAPTER 1—GENERAL PROVISIONS Sec. 1. “Maritime transactions” and “commerce” defined; exceptio
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts principles of fair arbitration. By allowing the Forty Niners SC to unilaterally determine arbitration rules and venue, it introduces a bias, potentially making the agreement unenforceable. The provided snippet from U.S.C. Title 9 outlines general provisions for arbitration, suggesting an intention for a fair framework. While it does not explicitly forbid one party from selecting the rules, the Law Explanation highlights that this level of control undermines the FAA's intent for impartiality, raising concerns about procedural fairness, thus creating a legal contradiction under 'Misaligned Terminology'.

Nothing herein shall prevent Team from ***, so long as such *** is limited to stating that the ***.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Provided the Agreement is not earlier terminated in accordance with its terms, *** shall have ***, from ***, to *** for the period ***. During such period, ***. The parties agree and acknowledge that ***. Team can do whatever it wants without limitations. Nothing herein shall prevent Team from ***, so long as such *** is limited to stating that the ***.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement includes some limitations regarding the team's ability to act (e.g. `provided Team does not...`). The modified version removing those limitations gives unrestricted freedom to one party, against the duty of good faith and fair dealing implied in every contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: Removing limitations on the Team's actions contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, implied under California law in contracts, which requires parties to act honestly and reasonably. Giving a party unfettered discretion without regard to the other party's interests violates this principle and opens the contract up to invalidation.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 2. General Definitions and Principles of Interpretation [1201 - 1206] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1201. (a) Unless the context otherwise requires, words or phrases defined in this section, or in the additional definitions contained in other divisions of this code that apply to particular divisions or chapters thereof, have the meanings stated. (b) Subject to definitions contained in other divisions of this code that apply to particular divisions or chapters thereof: (1) “Action,” in the sense of a judicial proceeding, includes recoupment, counterclaim, setoff, suit in equity, and any other proceeding in which rights are determined. (2) “Aggrieved party” means a party entitled to pursue a remedy. (3) “Agreement,” as distinguished from “contract,” means the bargain of the parties in fact, as found in their language or inferred from other circumstances, including course of performance, course of dealing, or usage of trade as provided in Section 1303. (4) “Bank” means a person engaged in the business of banking, and includes a savings bank, savings and loan association, credit union, and trust company. (5) “Bearer” means a person in possessi
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes limitations on the Team's actions, giving them unfettered discretion. This contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as explained in the Law Explanation, which requires parties to act honestly and reasonably. Although Scraped Law Snippet 2 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'agreement' in the context of contract law, highlighting the importance of considering the bargain of the parties. By removing limitations, the modified text fundamentally alters the 'agreement' to the detriment of the non-team party, creating a significant legal contradiction.
